title: Race-Lap Shared Event Signals
summary: Safety Car/VSC or race-wide disruption can change pit incentives by reducing pit-loss cost; even without explicit flags, shared lap-time anomalies across many drivers can proxy these events.
rationale: Safety Car/VSC or race-wide disruption can change pit incentives by reducing pit-loss cost; even without explicit flags, shared lap-time anomalies across many drivers can proxy these events.
implementation_hint: Within each Race-Year-LapNumber, compute median LapTime, median LapTime_Delta, count of large slowdowns, lap-time dispersion, and each driver's deviation from race-lap median/rank. Add lagged race-lap anomaly features to avoid using same-lap artefacts too aggressively.
expected_effect: Captures synchronized pit-stop hazard spikes that individual driver features miss.
risk: May overfit if test race distributions differ; same-lap global aggregates should be validated with GroupKFold by race/year.
